title: Epitaph On The Same (On The Death Of Demar, The Usurer)
content:
Beneath this verdant hillock lies
    Demar, the wealthy and the wise,
    His heirs,[1] that he might safely rest,
    Have put his carcass in a chest;
    The very chest in which, they say,
    His other self, his money, lay.
    And, if his heirs continue kind
    To that dear self he left behind,
    I dare believe, that four in five
    Will think his better self alive.
meaning:
"Epitaph On The Same (On The Death Of Demar, The Usurer)" is a quintessential example of Jonathan Swift's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
